sourceRef: tip:Training time conversions without the headache

slides.headline: Convert Training Time Hassle-Free
slides.body: Understand the mental model to effortlessly convert training time across VA chapters.

slides.headline: Understand the Core
slides.body: Know the basic rule: training time converts at 1 annual payment per 15 hours.

slides.headline: Apply to Chapter 33
slides.body: For Chapter 33, add 10% for each additional 15 hours. Example: 150 hours = 10 payments + 10% = 11 payments.

slides.headline: Chapter 31 and 35
slides.body: For Chapter 31 and 35, use the 85/15 rule: 85% of training time converts directly. Example: 150 hours = 127.5 payments.

slides.headline: Chapter 31 and 35 (cont.)
slides.body: For Chapter 31 and 35, if training time exceeds 100 hours, apply the 85/15 rule to the first 100 hours and then 10% to the remainder. Example: 150 hours = 85 payments + 10% of 50 = 94.5 payments.

slides.headline: Chapter 33 (cont.)
slides.body: For Chapter 33, if training time exceeds 100 hours, apply the 85/15 rule to the first 100 hours and then 10% to the remainder, plus an additional 10% for the excess hours. Example: 150 hours = 85 payments + 10% of 50 = 94.5 payments + 10% of 50 = 103.5 payments.
Master these rules with practice. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Understand the mental model for training time conversions across VA chapters. Easy-to-follow steps for every chapter. Master these rules with practice. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 3 of 5, which could benefit from a more concise headline to improve readability.
